dashboard.subtitle: Your operator cockpit: system health, quick access, and setup.

health.heading: System health
health.subtitle: Live status of the core services. Select a card to manage it.

middleware.title: Middleware
middleware.downDetail: No response from the middleware API.

llm.title: LLM provider
llm.connected: {count, plural, one {# provider connected} other {# providers connected}}

orchestrators.available: {count, plural, one {# orchestrator available} other {# orchestrators available}}

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.installed: {count, plural, one {# plugin installed} other {# plugins installed}}

quick.heading: Quick access
quick.subtitle: Jump straight into the most-used surfaces.

chat.title: Chat
chat.description: Talk to your orchestrators and run tasks.

hub.title: Hub
hub.description: Browse and install plugins for your team.

onboarding.chooseCaseSubtitle: Pick your role and we'll recommend matching plugins, grouped by area.

sales.name: Sales & CRM
sales.description: Reach customers across channels, with CRM and ERP data at hand.

hr.name: HR & Recruiting
hr.description: Answer HR questions and run people processes from chat.

finance.name: Finance & Accounting
finance.description: Bookkeeping and finance workflows backed by your ERP.

devteam.name: Software team / DevOps
devteam.description: Ship faster with dev tools, docs, and team channels.

recommend.catalogUnavailable: Plugin catalog unavailable right now. Try again in a moment.
recommend.subtitle: Recommended plugins for this business case.

llmStep.title: Connect an LLM
llmStep.description: Without a connected LLM no orchestrator can run. Connect an API provider or a subscription CLI first.

onboarding.heading: Get started
onboarding.subtitle: Pick a role to install a recommended set of plugins. You can fine-tune everything later.

bringSkills.title: Bring your skills
bringSkills.subtitle: Already have skills you trust — e.g. from Claude? Import one to get an early win with your own work. You can skip this.
bringSkills.imported: Imported "{name}" — now build an agent that uses it.

onboarding.empty: No role profiles available. Connect a registry to see recommendations.

adminLlm.title: LLM access
adminLlm.subtitle: Give the orchestrator a model: connect an API provider and assign models per plugin, or log in to a vendor subscription CLI.

index.subtitle: Configure and monitor agents, models, knowledge, plugins, access, and cost for this omadia instance.

llmAccess.title: LLM access
llmAccess.description: Connect LLM providers (API keys), assign models per plugin, and log in to subscription CLIs.

usage.title: Usage & Cost
usage.description: Track token usage and cost per model, source and time, with cache-hit rate across every call.

providers.title: Providers
providers.description: Connect providers and assign a provider plus model per agent; keys stay encrypted in the vault.

subscriptionClis.title: Subscription CLIs
subscriptionClis.description: Run agents on an existing LLM subscription (Claude Pro/Max) via the official CLI instead of an API key.

kgLifecycle.title: Knowledge-Graph Lifecycle
kgLifecycle.description: Inspect tier, decay and scope distribution, and run decay and GC sweeps manually.

kgPriorities.title: Knowledge-Graph Priorities
kgPriorities.description: Set per-agent block and boost lists that override recall hits in the token-budget assembler.

bulkPromote.title: Bulk Promotion
bulkPromote.description: Re-score historical turns and promote high-significance ones to durable knowledge (idempotent).

inconsistencies.title: Contradictions
inconsistencies.description: Resolve semantically similar memories whose statements conflict; the operator decides which holds.

memoryBackend.title: Storage Backend
memoryBackend.description: Switch memory storage between filesystem and Postgres; Postgres needs DATABASE_URL and takes effect after a restart.

domains.title: Domains
domains.description: Browse registered plugins grouped by domain (read-only).

registries.title: Registries
registries.description: Manage store sources, including private registries with tokens; changes apply without a restart.

mcp.title: MCP Control Center
mcp.description: MCP servers, discovered tools with scan verdicts, grants, and the call audit log in one place.

auth.title: Authentication Providers
auth.description: Enable or disable local login and Entra ID sign-in; changes apply without a restart.

users.title: Users
users.description: Create, disable, reset passwords for, or delete local accounts.

dangerZone.title: Memory Purge
dangerZone.description: Irreversibly delete memory along one axis (all, agent, user, team, channel); preview-gated with a confirm phrase, no undo.

builder.title: Agent Builder
builder.subtitle: Wire channels, sub-agents, skills and tools on an editable canvas.

card.title: Agent Builder
card.description: Visual node-graph canvas: wire channels, sub-agents, skills, tools/MCP and schedules. Drawing a connection writes the wiring; deleting an edge removes it.

import.title: Import a skill
import.hint: Drop or paste a Claude SKILL.md, a Codex AGENTS.md, or a ChatGPT export. Only the text is imported — bundled scripts must be added as a plugin.

outcome.created: Will be created as a new skill.
outcome.updated: Will update the existing imported skill.

risks.title: Review before importing — this content looks like it may try to:

code.system_prompt_reference: Reassign the agent's role / system prompt
code.tool_coercion: Force tool calls or bypass confirmation
code.credential_harvest: Collect API keys, passwords, or secrets
code.silent_permission_escalation: Request broader access without telling the user

inspector.title: Inspector
inspector.ackDone: Risk acknowledged, tool can be granted
inspector.readOnly: This node has no editable properties.
inspector.name: Name

persona.title: Direct-answer personas
persona.hintEmpty: No persona skills attached — this Agent answers with the generic default identity.
persona.hintSome: The Agent picks one of these per turn to answer as directly, or the default identity if none clearly fit — no sub-agent hop.
persona.noSkillsAvailable: No more skills available to attach.
persona.riskWarning: Heuristic scan flagged this skill: {codes}. As a persona it drives the top-level agent with full tool access — review before relying on it.
persona.confirmAttachHighRisk: This skill's heuristic scan is at high risk ({codes}). As a persona it drives the top-level agent with full tool access. Confirm you've reviewed it before attaching.

selfExtension.intro: Propose a self-extension for this installed plugin: add tools or capabilities by patching its spec. The escalation guard auto-denies any privilege widening — a plugin can never grant itself rights it does not already hold.
selfExtension.patchesPlaceholder: Spec patches as a JSON array, e.g. [{"op":"add","path":"/tools/-","value":{"id":"my_tool","description":"…","input":{}}}]
selfExtension.invalidPatchesJson: Patches must be a valid JSON array of RFC-6902 operations.
selfExtension.invalidParamsJson: Params must be a valid JSON object.

conductor.title: Conductor
conductor.intro: Build and run deterministic workflows that combine Agents, actions and human steps. The runtime — not the model — owns step progression.
conductor.noWorkflows: No workflows yet. Publish one below to get started.
conductor.publishHint: The graph is validated by the engine before it is saved. An agent step's agentId is the slug of an Agent (orchestrator), e.g. "fallback".
conductor.designerHint: Add steps, drag between them to wire transitions, edit a step in the inspector, then Save. Mark one step as the entry.
conductor.chatHint: Describe the workflow you want; the builder proposes graph edits each turn. Use "Show in designer" to open the draft in the visual canvas below.
conductor.chatEmpty: Describe the process you want to automate to get started.
conductor.chatPlaceholder: e.g. When a PR is merged, ask the on-call engineer to review, then post to the team channel.
conductor.chatNeedSlug: Set a slug and a name to publish.
conductor.eventCatalogHint: {count} declared event(s) available — pick one or type a custom id.
conductor.eventCatalogEmpty: No events declared by installed plugins yet — type a custom id.
conductor.inspectorEmpty: Select a step or transition to edit it.
conductor.emitHint: Fire a domain event — every enabled workflow with a matching event trigger (id + payload filter) starts a run. This is the Conductor Surface a connector calls.
conductor.dryRunHint: Simulated — human steps auto-approved inline, connector actions stubbed, no durable awaits or notifications.
conductor.rolesHint: A human step can address a role (role:<key>) instead of a fixed person. The role resolves live to its current holder(s) — moving the baton re-routes pending and future awaits.
conductor.quorumAnyHint: The step continues as soon as one holder responds.
conductor.quorumAllHint: Every current holder must respond before the step continues.
conductor.cronPreviewMonthly: On day {dom} of every month at {time}
conductor.agentSlugHint: The orchestrator slug to run for this step.
conductor.actionIdHint: The tool / connector action id to invoke.
conductor.kvHint: Tip: wrap text in quotes to keep it a string, e.g. "123".

operatorChannels.title: Channels
operatorChannels.subtitle: Route inbound webhooks per channel-key to an Orchestrator. Each channel plugin reports its own keys — you only pick which Orchestrator answers them.
operatorChannels.emptyExplain: Channel plugins (Teams, Telegram, …) report their keys during activation. As soon as a channel plugin is installed and active, its keys appear here.
operatorChannels.fallbackHint: Channels without an explicit binding fall through to the standard Orchestrator ({fallback}).

layout.metaDescription: Admin UI for the omadia agent platform (local).

login.title: Sign in to omadia
login.noProviders: No auth providers are configured. Set {envVar} in the middleware environment and restart.

setup.title: Welcome to omadia
setup.intro: Create the first administrator account. You can invite more users from the admin UI afterwards.
setup.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
setup.alreadyLocked: Setup is already locked — another user has been created.
setup.credentialsRejected: Email or password rejected by the server (must be valid email + 8+ chars).
setup.anthropicKeyHelp: Starts with sk-ant-…. Get one at console.anthropic.com. Stored encrypted in the per-plugin vault — never in .env. Leave empty to add later via Admin → Runtime → Secrets.
setup.anthropicKeyInvalid: Anthropic API keys start with "sk-ant-". Double-check the value from console.anthropic.com.
setup.anthropicKeyRejected: The server rejected this Anthropic key (401/403). Verify it at console.anthropic.com → API keys.
setup.providerHint: No LLM key needed here. After you create the admin account, connect a provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, …) under Admin → LLM providers.

onboarding.title: Pick a profile — operational in <60 seconds
onboarding.intro: This middleware is still empty ({count, plural, one {# plugin} other {# plugins}} installed). Pick a curated profile — the plugins will be registered in the correct capability order. Secrets like API keys are set afterwards via the wizard.
onboarding.exportSuffix: and import it into another deployment.
onboarding.secretsHint: Plugins that need operator secrets (e.g. anthropic_api_key, telegram_bot_token) start in status {erroredTag} until you set the secrets via the wizard on the respective detail page. Then activate via {reactivateTag}.

chatTabs.closeWhileBusyTitle: Wait until the turn is done

error.title: Something went wrong
error.description: This page hit an unexpected error and couldn't finish loading.
error.resetDataHint: Clears chats stored locally in this browser, then reloads. Your server-side data is untouched — use this if reloading alone doesn't help.

session.warningBody: Your session ends in {time}. Sign in again now so you are not interrupted in the middle of your work.
session.expiredBody: Your session has expired. Please sign in again to continue working.

agentDetailsModal.footerNote: Top-level tool calls only. Inner agent iterations are not counted as separate calls.

chat.errorMiddlewareUnreachable: Middleware unreachable on localhost:3979. Is `npm run dev` running in the middleware folder?
chat.errorProxyFailure: Proxy error (HTTP {status}). Check the middleware logs.

intro.body: This is the operator debug chat — a direct line to your orchestrators for testing prompts and inspecting tool calls, plans, and knowledge-graph walks as they happen. It's not the end-user experience; it's built for debugging.

chat.composerResetTitle: Reset chat (⌘⇧K) — history goes, memory stays
chat.resetConfirmBody: The conversation in this chat will be cleared and the agent forgets its current context. Memory and Knowledge-Graph entries are preserved.
chat.steerButtonTitle: Inject this message into the running turn (⌘↵ / Ctrl↵) — applied at the next step
chat.steerHint: Type to steer this turn — your message is folded in at the next step. Or press Stop.
chat.steerNoticeSent: Steering sent — it will be applied at the next step.
chat.steerNoticeEnded: The turn just finished — press Send to ask it as a new message.
chat.emptyStatePrompt: Ask the orchestrator — the answer streams live including tool calls.
chat.emptyStateShortcut: Shortcut: ⌘↵ / Ctrl↵ · new tab via „+ New chat"

autoPromoted.label: Saved as {kind} by Palaia
autoPromoted.discardTitle: Delete this auto-saved memory — the manual save button comes back
autoPromoted.discardConfirm: Discard the auto-saved memory? The manual save button will come back so you can re-save with your own classification.

saveAsMemory.buttonTitle: Promote this turn into a curated MemorableKnowledge entry
saveAsMemory.summaryPlaceholder: Short summary of what this memory captures…
saveAsMemory.excerptInsertTitle: Insert this excerpt into the summary

source.llmTitle: Pre-filled by the Palaia excerpt extractor

saveAsMemory.rationalePlaceholder: Why is this memorable? Context the agent should keep…
saveAsMemory.successHeadline: Saved. The memory is now visible on /memories.

kgWalk.title: Knowledge graph
kgWalk.subtitle: Neighborhood this turn surfaced
kgWalk.empty: No graph walk for this turn yet.
kgWalk.hopListHeader: {nodes, plural, one {# node} other {# nodes}} · {hops, plural, one {# hop} other {# hops}}

planDag.title: Plan
planDag.subtitle: Live plan this turn is executing

privacyReceipt.summaryDatasets: {count, plural, one {# tool result} other {# tool results}} processed server-side
privacyReceipt.summaryMasked: {count, plural, one {# field masked} other {# fields masked}}
privacyReceipt.summaryIdentityOnWire: ⚠ {count, plural, one {# name to the model} other {# names to the model}}
privacyReceipt.explainer: Raw tool results stayed server-side behind the data-plane boundary — the model only ever saw an identity-free digest.
privacyReceipt.explainerBreach: You named a person in your request — that name reached the model in cleartext. The data fetched from tools stayed server-side behind the boundary.
privacyReceipt.summaryBypassed: {count, plural, one {# tool not masked} other {# tools not masked}}
privacyReceipt.explainerBypassed: The operator set Privacy Mode to »Bypass« for at least one plugin — its tool results reached the model unmasked. Used for trusted internal sources whose content the Privacy Shield cannot usefully digest structurally.

directLine.delegatedAnswerCaption: Delivered verbatim — the orchestrator cannot edit or remove this answer.

privacyOperator.heroLede: Inspect what the shield is detecting, tune the allowlist, and live-test inputs against the current detector pool.
privacyOperator.errorLoadingState: Failed to load the privacy state.
privacyOperator.tenantSelfSubtitle: Terms naming the tenant itself; tokenising them would mask the conversation's primary subject.
privacyOperator.tenantSelfFooter: Source: tenant_self_terms plugin config. Auto-fill from operator profile lands in v0.2.x.
privacyOperator.repoDefaultSubtitle: German office / HR topic-nouns shipped with the plugin to suppress NER false positives.
privacyOperator.overrideSubtitle: Free-form additions you want passed through unmasked. One term per line.
privacyOperator.overrideRuntimeOnly: ⚠ Runtime-only — changes revert on restart. Durable persistence lands in a follow-up slice.
privacyOperator.liveTestSubtitle: Run the full detector + allowlist + tokenise pipeline on arbitrary text. No persistence; just visibility.
privacyOperator.liveTestPlaceholder: Paste text to inspect what the shield would do…
privacyOperator.liveTestNoDetectorHits: No detections — payload passes through unchanged.
privacyOperator.liveTestNoAllowlistMatches: No terms suppressed by the allowlist.
privacyOperator.auditSubtitle: Forensic timeline of receipts emitted across turns.
privacyOperator.auditDeferred: Available in v0.2.x — requires receipt persistence.

agentPicker.label: Orchestrator:
agentPicker.pinnedTooltip: Session is pinned. Use Operator → Orchestrators → Drain to reassign.
agentPicker.emptyCta: No orchestrators configured — open Operator → Orchestrators
agentPicker.unavailableTitle: This session's Orchestrator is no longer available
agentPicker.unavailableBody: The Orchestrator "{slug}" was deleted or disabled. Pick how to recover this session.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Your skill library — import, view, edit and export skills used across agents.
skills.selectHint: Select a skill to view and edit it.
skills.scopeHint: This manages your shared skill library. Imported or edited skills land in the registry; attach them to this agent via its slots.
skills.confirmDelete: This skill is used by {count} agents — they will lose it. Delete anyway?
skills.usedByPersona: Also the direct-answer persona for {count} agents

editor.name: Name
editor.forkNotice: This is an imported skill. Saving creates an editable copy and keeps the original as its source.

capabilities.heading: Required tools
capabilities.bindWarning: When this skill is active, including when it is auto-selected for a turn, it may use server "{server}" with the least privilege of this binding.

verdict.why: Heuristic scan flagged: {codes}. This is a signal, not proof the content is safe.

riskCode.system_prompt_reference: Reassign the agent's role / system prompt
riskCode.tool_coercion: Force tool calls or bypass confirmation
riskCode.credential_harvest: Collect API keys, passwords, or secrets
riskCode.silent_permission_escalation: Request broader access without telling the user

operatorAgents.title: Orchestrators
operatorAgents.subtitle: Multi-orchestrator runtime — create, edit, disable, drain, or kill Orchestrators and watch the registry react in real time via the LISTEN/NOTIFY hot-reload bus.
operatorAgents.agentsEmpty: No orchestrators yet. Create one above.
operatorAgents.drainTooltip: Clear snapshot on every session bound to this Orchestrator — next turn re-captures from current config
operatorAgents.fallbackProtected: This is the standard orchestrator and cannot be disabled or deleted.
operatorAgents.killConfirm: Kill all sessions for "{slug}"? History will be lost.
operatorAgents.rehydrateTooltip: Re-attaches every installed plugin to the current standard Orchestrator — restores day-1 capabilities.
operatorAgents.rehydrateConfirm: Re-attach every installed plugin to the standard Orchestrator? Existing plugin configs are preserved; newly added plugins start with empty config.
operatorAgents.catalogError: Failed to load the plugin catalog ({message}). The multi-select falls back to manual mode.
operatorAgents.multiInstanceFalseBadge: multi_instance: false — may be active on at most ONE orchestrator
operatorAgents.bindingsEmpty: No bindings — unbound traffic lands on the standard Orchestrator.
operatorAgents.routingTesterHelp: Ask the server which Orchestrator would handle an inbound webhook. Hits explicit bindings first, then the standard orchestrator.
operatorAgents.routingTesterNoMatch: No match — and no standard orchestrator configured.
operatorAgents.pluginsAvailableEmpty: All installed plugins are already attached.
operatorAgents.pluginsEnabledEmpty: Drag-and-drop or click "Attach" to add plugins.
operatorAgents.dependencyMissingTooltip: This plugin depends on "{parent}" — parent is not attached in the same column.
operatorAgents.fallbackStoreOnlyNotice: The standard Orchestrator always runs plugins with the global store config. Per-Orchestrator overrides are disabled here — create a dedicated Orchestrator if you need them.
operatorAgents.orphanDetachAllTooltip: Detach every stale plugin row (old ids from earlier deployments) at once.
operatorAgents.orphanExplain: Plugin ids from earlier seeds that no longer exist in the current catalog (typically a rename like de.byte5.agent.* → @omadia/*). Dropped on next Save unless you tick "Keep".

streamToasts.abortConfirmBody: The running response will be aborted immediately and can't be resumed.

issueReport.publicWarning: This text goes into the public repo. Secrets and personal data were removed automatically — please review before filing.
issueReport.errorNoApp: Direct filing is not configured on this instance. Please report manually via the GitHub tab.
issueReport.errorInvalidNumber: Please enter a valid issue number.

intro.title: Let's build your assistant
intro.subtitle: In three calm steps — describe it, review it, try it out. The technical side takes care of itself.

step1.title: Describe your assistant
step1.subtitle: Say in your own words what it should do for you.

step2.title: Your assistant
step2.subtitle: An overview in plain language — no jargon.

step3.title: Try it out
step3.subtitle: Ask a question and see how your assistant replies.

overview.whatPlaceholder: Once you've described above what your assistant should do, a short summary will appear here.
overview.readyTools: Ready. Can carry out {count, plural, one {# action} other {# actions}} for you.
overview.readyChat: Ready. Answers your questions in the chat.

persona.title: Adjust personality
persona.subtitle: How should your assistant sound? Friendly, factual, casual …

intake.heroTitle: What should your assistant be able to do?
intake.heroSubtitle: Just describe it in your own words. We'll handle the rest — you don't need to know anything technical.
intake.example1: An assistant that answers questions about our Confluence pages.
intake.example2: A helper that summarizes incoming emails.
intake.example3: An assistant for questions about our vacation policy.
intake.placeholderIdle: Describe in your own words what your assistant should do.
intake.errorChoiceFailed: Your choice could not be saved.
intake.errorChoiceFailedMessage: Your choice could not be saved: {message}

workspace.viewModeSimpleTitle: Guided no-code view — describe, review, try out
workspace.viewModeExtendedTitle: Full builder — spec, slots, source code, build status
workspace.viewModeLockedTitle: Please wait for the current reply to finish before switching views
workspace.installEditorWarnings: {count, plural, one {# editor warning} other {# editor warnings}}
workspace.installMissingCredentials: {count, plural, one {# missing test credential} other {# missing test credentials}}
workspace.templateTooltipEnabled: Switch template (lossless — only slots / tools / depends_on are template-specific)
workspace.templateTooltipDisabled: Template switch disabled — the draft already has slots, tools or `depends_on` entries that would be lost on switch. Create a new draft or clear those fields first.
workspace.autoFixTitleOn: Auto-Fix active: build/smoke failures automatically trigger a builder turn
workspace.autoFixTitleOff: Auto-Fix off: failures only show the "Fix with Builder" button
workspace.autoFixStoppedBody: The builder agent can't get past the same class of error. Auto-Fix was switched off automatically — review the code manually or turn the toggle back on once you've intervened.

form.descriptionPlaceholder: What does this tool do? Read by the agent as the description.

list.stuckTitle: Builder agent got stuck on this tool/slot — check manually

bulkImport.title: Import tools
bulkImport.previewEmpty: Paste input to see the preview.
bulkImport.invalidJson: Input is not valid JSON. YAML support is coming in the B.11+ cleanup.
bulkImport.collisionNote: Collisions are skipped — existing tools stay unchanged.

test.subtitle: Direct call against the current preview instance.
test.dismissHint: Esc or a click outside closes the modal.
test.emptyInput: Tool accepts empty input — press Run.
test.arrayJsonHint: Array — JSON syntax, commits on blur.
test.objectJsonHint: Object — JSON syntax, commits on blur.

schema.itemPropertiesHint: Item properties are edited in the nested builder below (depth {depth}).
schema.topLevelMustBeObject: Top level must be an object.
schema.jsonModeHint: Direct JSON-schema mode. When switching to the form view, unsupported patterns (oneOf, $ref, polymorphic) become read-only.
schema.noProperties: No properties — tool accepts an empty input object.

validation.idEmpty: Tool ID must not be empty
validation.idFormat: Tool ID must be snake_case (lowercase letters, digits, underscores; must start with a letter)
validation.idMissing: id missing or not a string
validation.descriptionMissing: description missing or not a string

placeholder.idle: Describe what the agent should do. Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a line break.

empty.title: Tell the builder what you want to start with.
empty.example: e.g. „Build a Confluence reader agent with the tools page_read and page_search.“

chat.credentialsTooltip: Manage test credentials for the preview

placeholder.idle: Test the agent. Press Enter to send.

error.unknownTurn: Unknown error during the preview turn

chat.externalReadsHint: Preview does not run cross-integration lookups (spec.external_reads) — the agent correctly detects the missing service and throws. Publish the agent to the platform store to test the real data path.

agentStuck.title: Builder agent is stuck on slot {slot} ({attempts, plural, one {# attempt} other {# attempts}})
agentStuck.body: {summary}. Open the slot editor, review the errors manually and fix the code — the agent won't retry on its own.

empty.title: Test the preview agent.
empty.body: Spec + slots are recompiled before each turn if anything changed.

secrets.title: Test credentials

persistence.persistent: Values are stored encrypted in the vault and survive a restart. Clicking «Clear all» wipes them permanently.
persistence.ephemeral: Values live only in the server's memory and are gone on logout/restart.
persistence.unknown: Values are stored server-side in the PreviewSecretBuffer.
persistence.notForProduction: Not for production — installed plugins pull their credentials from the vault via RequiresWizard.

secrets.noFields: No setup_fields declared. Add some in the spec tab to test credentials.

placeholder.buffered: set — leave empty to keep the value

array.malformedTooltip: Entry is not a string — shown as best-effort

setupFields.emptyState: No setup fields yet. Add e.g. <example>api_key (secret, required)</example> so the preview agent can read test credentials.
setupFields.descriptionPlaceholder: What does the agent need this field for?

validation.keyFormat: Key must be snake_case (a-z, 0-9, _; start with a letter).

oauth.noProviders: Declare an oauth_providers descriptor first (ask the builder agent) — then pick it here.

empty.title: No slots filled yet.
empty.hint: Let the Builder fill the slots, or write them yourself.

slots.askAgentTooltip: Sends the prompt straight to the Builder chat — the agent gets started right away.
slots.prefillPrompt: Fill the slot `{slotKey}` (target: {targetFile}) with the TypeScript implementation.
slots.prefillPromptWithDescription: Fill the slot `{slotKey}` (target: {targetFile}) — {description} with the TypeScript implementation.

pillar.heading: Shape character
pillar.description: 12 axes, each individually optional. Values are mirrored in the AGENT.md frontmatter — Phase 4 compiles them into a persona section in the system prompt.
pillar.customNotesPlaceholder: e.g. "Reply in German when the user writes in German."

gallery.title: Choose a persona template
gallery.customDescription: Apply no template — use your own axes.

customerService.label: Customer Service Agent
customerService.description: Professional support with empathy and clear escalation.

salesDev.label: Sales Development Rep
salesDev.description: Proactive sales with a personal touch and clear follow-up.

contentMarketing.label: Content & Marketing Agent
contentMarketing.description: Creative brand voice with strategic thinking.

softwareEngineer.label: Software Engineer
softwareEngineer.description: Team-oriented developer with a clear communication style.

teamLead.label: Team Lead / Coordinator
teamLead.description: Orchestrator managing delegation, escalation and team structure.

culture.label: Industry / Culture
culture.noChange: No change — all values already match.

conflicts.sycophancyHighDirectnessLow: Sycophancy=high enforces devil's-advocate rules; directness ≤ 30 says "diplomatic". The model will ignore one of the two. Recommendation: directness ≥ 50 or sycophancy=medium.
conflicts.sycophancyHighDirectnessHigh: Amplification — critical evaluation plus no politeness buffer can come across as combative.
conflicts.sycophancyOffDirectnessVeryHigh: Note: directness ≥ 90 alone does not override the model's default flattery behaviour. For anti-sycophancy, deliberately pick a sycophancy level.
conflicts.formalityHighHumorHigh: Very formal + very humorous comes across as stylistically inconsistent.
conflicts.sarcasmHighWarmthHigh: High sarcasm + high warmth — replies come across as contradictory.

formality.description: Casual address & colloquial language vs. formal address & technical language.

directness.description: Politeness buffer vs. key point first.

warmth.description: Matter-of-fact and distanced vs. empathetic & appreciative.

sarcasm.description: Honest and direct vs. ironic (rarely appropriate).

proactivity.description: Responds to requests vs. makes suggestions on its own.

adminUi.title: Admin UI
adminUi.subtitle: An iframe in the store detail, operator-only. For maintaining data that does not come from the API.
adminUi.toggleHelp: Plugin mounts an HTML page at admin_ui_path. The Web UI embeds it as an <iframe> on the store detail page after install.
adminUi.bodyHint: Content between the marker regions. Inline style + script allowed.
adminUi.operatorContract: Operator contract: Frontend fetches must use relative URLs (fetch('api/devices'), not fetch('/api/...')) — the UI runs behind a /bot-api/* rewrite. JSON responses need the { ok: boolean, ... } schema, otherwise the RuntimeSmoke flags them as broken.
adminUi.disabledHint: Off. Plugins without admin_ui_path have no admin tile on the store detail page — that is the default.
adminUi.stubEditHint: Edit this block via fill_slot('admin-ui-body', ...) or directly in the Workspace.

dashboardPages.title: Dashboard Pages
dashboardPages.subtitle: Routes rendered as browser/Teams tabs. For end-user-facing displays (PR inbox, KPIs, lists).

tab.heading: UI Surfaces
tab.description: Expose plugin UI outward: one admin iframe for operator-maintained data (single), and 0–N dashboard pages for end-user-facing browser/Teams tab displays.

fields.routeIdHelp: Lowercase, hyphens allowed. Determines slot keys + filename.
fields.pathHelp: Relative to the plugin mount. Final: /p/<id><path>.
fields.tabLabelHelp: Max 24 characters. Shown in the Teams tab + hub card.
fields.pageTitleHelp: Full page title in the HTML doc. Max 80 characters.

renderMode.heading: Render Mode
renderMode.libraryDesc: Curated Tailwind templates (list-card / kpi-tiles). Fast, no build step.
renderMode.reactSsrDesc: TSX component (server-rendered). Full React DX, server-rendered, Tailwind via CDN.
renderMode.freeFormHtmlDesc: Free html`…` slot. You write the Express route body yourself.

uiTemplate.heading: UI Template
uiTemplate.listCardDesc: List of cards (title + subtitle + url). Default for "show me X items".
uiTemplate.kpiTilesDesc: 1–4 number tiles (label + value + optional hint). For counts/KPIs.

dataBinding.heading: Data Source
dataBinding.help: The selected tool is called with empty input on every page request; its output flows as props into the render function.

interactive.label: Interactive (Hydration)
interactive.help: SSR + client-side hydration via esm.sh import map. Set to true when your component uses useState, onClick, etc. The root element MUST carry data-omadia-page="{routeId}".

componentSlot.label: Component (TSX, default-export)
componentSlot.hint: Props: { data: unknown; fetchError: string | null }

itemTemplate.heading: Item Template (path interpolation: item.fieldname)
itemTemplate.suffixHelp: Whitelisted suffixes: .join(", ") for arrays, .toLocaleString() for numbers. Other function calls are ignored.

pagesList.emptyHint: A page becomes a /p/<plugin>/<path> endpoint and a hub card for Teams.

hints.previewCold: Click "Build" or send a chat message — once the preview is warm (green build status), the iframe loads automatically.
hints.uiRouteNotFound: The routeId does not exist in the current spec. Save your page first (same form), then try the reload again.
hints.previewCaptureMissing: The warm preview was built from a spec without ui_routes. Trigger a build (chat or "Rebuild") after creating the page.
hints.previewProxyFailed: The temporary probe server could not load the plugin route. Check the console: the component probably throws during SSR.
hints.fetchFailed: Network error while loading the preview. Try refresh.

versions.relDaysAgo: {count, plural, one {# day} other {# days}} ago
versions.emptyStateHint: No snapshots yet. “Create snapshot” pins the current state so you can roll back to it later.
versions.downloadPluginTitle: Plugin ZIP — directly installable via /install/packages/upload (incl. AGENT.md with persona/quality)
versions.downloadBundleTitle: Profile bundle — for cross-instance migration via /api/v1/profiles/import-bundle
versions.toastRollbackDone: Rollback complete · {count, plural, one {# file} other {# files}} restored
versions.vendorBundleLabel: Pack plugin ZIPs into the bundle (air-gap export)
versions.rollbackBodySuffix: + knowledge files are reset to the snapshot state. Plugin pins stay unchanged.
versions.rollbackTypeHint: Type the first 12 characters of the bundle hash to confirm.

install.publishLeadPrefix: This surface will be written to the plugin store. After “Publish”,
install.publishLeadSuffix: is available to every agent in the tenant.
install.noSkillPromptSlot: No dedicated skill-prompt slot — the agent runs with the boilerplate default instruction.
install.sectionSetupFieldsSubtitle: to be filled in by the operator on activation
install.hintConflict: Delete the existing plugin in the store or change `id` / `version` in the spec.
install.hintBuildFailed: Go back to the slot editor and fix the spots the compiler flags.
install.hintCodegenFailed: The spec has missing required fields or unresolved placeholders — see the issue list.
install.hintSpecInvalid: The spec has a schema problem. Check required fields in the spec editor.
install.hintTooLarge: Reduce slot content or asset sizes — the server limit has been reached.
install.bumpInvalidSemver: Version '{version}' is not semver — adjust it manually in the spec editor.
install.typescriptErrors: {count, plural, one {# TypeScript error} other {# TypeScript errors}}
install.codegenIssues: {count, plural, one {# codegen issue} other {# codegen issues}}
install.successBody: is visible in the store. Redirecting …

common.missingRequiredFields: {count, plural, one {# required field missing} other {# required fields missing}}

list.headline: Build new plugins.
list.lede: A plugin can package deterministic code (the tools and integrations the platform runs on), the agents that put them to work, or both together. Draft here, iterate on several in parallel, and install with one click. Every draft stays in your personal workspace, visible only to you.

draft.headline: No drafts yet.
draft.hint: Click “New plugin” in the top right to create your first draft.

published.headline: Nothing published yet.
published.hint: Published plugins appear here once you have pushed a draft into the platform via the workspace.

deleted.headline: Trash is empty.
deleted.hint: Deleted drafts show up here and can be restored until you delete them permanently.

create.label: New plugin
create.quotaReachedTooltip: Draft quota reached — delete an existing one before creating a new draft.

modal.title: Import bundle
modal.description: Drop a profile bundle ZIP (export from the Versions tab via ?format=bundle) here — we automatically create a new draft.
modal.dropzone: Drag a ZIP here or click
modal.overwriteLabel: Overwrite existing live content (only meaningful for profile targets — builder drafts are always fresh).

errors.conflict: Profile already has diverging live content ({assets}). Enable “Overwrite” to replace it.

pluginDirectory.heading: Per-plugin settings
pluginDirectory.intro: Most configuration now lives with each plugin and is edited in its own settings panel. Only genuinely cross-plugin values (like the shared Anthropic key above) remain on this page.

adminSettings.title: Configuration
adminSettings.intro: All {envFile} values that are written to the config store or the secret vault at startup. Changes are saved automatically and the affected plugin is re-activated — they take effect immediately, without a restart. Secrets only show whether a value is set; the value itself is never displayed.
adminSettings.vaultUnavailable: Vault unavailable — secrets cannot be edited.

adminProviders.title: LLM providers
adminProviders.intro: Connect LLM providers and choose which one each agent runs on. Keys are stored encrypted in the vault and never leave your server. Anthropic is the default; switching an agent to another provider takes effect live.
adminProviders.vaultUnavailable: Vault unavailable — provider keys cannot be read.

providers.heading: Providers
providers.removeKeyConfirm: Remove the stored API key for {provider}? Agents assigned to it will stop working until a new key is added.

assignments.heading: Per-agent assignment
assignments.intro: Pin each LLM-capable plugin to a provider and model. Saving re-activates the plugin so the change applies immediately.
assignments.toolLessBlocked: needs tools — not for this role
assignments.avvDisclosure: Routing this agent to {provider} sends its prompt data to a third-party processor. Ensure a data-processing agreement (DSGVO Art. 28) is in place before using it for personal data.
assignments.euHostedNote: {provider} is hosted in the EU. An Art. 28 DSGVO processing agreement is still required, but there is no third-country transfer — unlike US-based providers.

adminSubscriptionClis.title: Subscription CLIs
adminSubscriptionClis.intro: Run agents on an LLM subscription you already pay for (such as Claude Pro or Max) by connecting the vendor's official CLI, instead of paying per token with an API key. omadia detects which CLIs are installed and logged in on this server.

explainer.heading: Subscription vs API key
explainer.body: An API key bills you per token. A subscription CLI runs on your existing plan: you log in once with the official tool and omadia reuses that session. This is the same sanctioned path the official CLI uses, not token extraction.
explainer.singleOperator: Subscription credits belong to one account and cannot be shared, so this fits a single-operator self-host. For a shared or production gateway, use an API key on the LLM providers page.

adminSubscriptionClis.selectModelLink: Once logged in, select a CLI model for an agent in LLM providers

status.installedNotLoggedIn: Installed but not logged in yet.
status.installedUnknown: Installed. Login status could not be confirmed.

connect.heading: Sign in to your subscription
connect.openHint: Open the Anthropic sign-in page and approve access:
connect.pasteHint: Copy the code shown after you approve, paste it here, and submit:
connect.stillPending: Not signed in yet. Open the link, approve access, then submit the code again.
connect.step1: Make sure the CLI is installed (bundled in the omadia image, or install it):
connect.step2: Log in with your subscription account:
connect.step3: Come back here and press Re-check. A logged-in CLI shows a green badge.

adminSubscriptionClis.needsVerificationNote: Subscription billing for this CLI is not confirmed yet. Signing in may meter the API instead of your subscription, so it is not recommended for omadia until verified.

createIssue.buttonTitle: Report a bug or suggest a feature
createIssue.descriptionLabel: Describe it in your own words
createIssue.descriptionPlaceholder: What happened, or what would you like to see? Any language is fine — we'll phrase it in English.
createIssue.previewHint: Drafted by your connected AI. Edit anything before filing.
createIssue.connectIntro: Connect your GitHub account to file this issue as yourself.
createIssue.deviceWaiting: Waiting for you to authorize on GitHub …
createIssue.deviceExpired: The code expired. Please try connecting again.
createIssue.notConfigured: GitHub issue filing isn't configured on this instance.
createIssue.errorReformulate: Couldn't phrase the issue. Try again or edit the text.
createIssue.errorLlm: No AI provider is connected to phrase the issue.
createIssue.errorRateLimited: Your AI provider hit a rate or quota limit. Try again later, or switch the provider in Settings.
createIssue.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

hero.headline: Plugins for the omadia platform.
hero.lead: The Hub is our catalog of ready-made plugins. It bundles integrations (credential containers), agents (domain capabilities) and channels (user channels like Teams and Telegram). Click a tile to see permissions, secrets and dependencies before you install.

page.unknownLoadError: Unknown error while loading the catalog.
page.emptyFilteredHint: Switch the category or the source above.
page.emptyInstalledBody: Switch to the <hubLink>Hub</hubLink> or <localLink>Local</localLink> tab to browse and install available plugins.
page.emptyLocalHint: Upload a plugin package via drag & drop above — the server validates the manifest and registers it in the catalog.
page.emptyHubTitle: No plugins available in the Hub.
page.emptyHubBody: Connect a registry under <adminLink>Admin · Registries</adminLink> — or upload your own package in the <localLink>Local</localLink> tab.
page.errorTitle: The catalog could not be loaded.
page.errorHint: Check that the middleware is reachable at <host>http://localhost:3979</host> and that the endpoint <endpoint>/api/v1/store/plugins</endpoint> responds.

permissions.flagRuntimeSecrets: Writes its own credentials at runtime
permissions.flagCredentialFlows: Runs credential flows on its own routes

capabilities.installHint: During install the middleware checks these prerequisites transitively. If a provider is missing, a wizard opens with the matching plugins from the catalog.

install.confirmRemove: Remove {name}? The tool is immediately removed from the orchestrator, the vault namespace is cleared, and the registry entry is deleted.
install.deletePackageLabel: Also remove the uploaded package file under <mono>.uploaded-packages/</mono>. <subtle>Left unchecked, the package stays in the catalog and can be reinstalled — only built-ins are unaffected.</subtle>
install.noConfigNeeded: This plugin requires no configuration — confirm the installation at the bottom right.
install.secretsNote1: Secrets are stored exclusively in the per-agent vault.
install.secretsNote2: Other values go into the instance configuration.
install.privacyLoadFailed: Privacy mode could not be loaded.
install.privacyLoadFailedWithError: Privacy mode could not be loaded: {error}
install.privacyGuardedHelp: Tool results are masked server-side behind Privacy Shield v4; the LLM only sees identity-free digests.
install.privacyBypassHelp: Raw tool results reach the LLM unmasked. Suitable for trusted internal sources whose content the Privacy Shield cannot structurally digest (e.g. Confluence page bodies).
install.privacyPerToolHelp: Per-tool whitelist: only explicitly listed tools are bypassed. Set the list via the `_privacy_bypass_scopes` field (comma-separated).

auditMode.singleHostHelp: Only the hosts declared in the manifest.
auditMode.allowlistHelp: Manifest hosts plus the operator-maintained host list.
auditMode.publicWebHelp: Any public host. Private network ranges and cloud-metadata endpoints stay blocked.
auditMode.confirmPublicWeb: Public-Web allows this plugin to contact any public host. Private network ranges and cloud-metadata endpoints stay blocked. Continue?
auditMode.confirmAllowlist: Allowlist additionally allows this plugin to reach the operator-maintained host list. Continue?
auditMode.intro: Controls which hosts this audit plugin may reach via ctx.http. Default is Single-Host.

credentials.noFields: This plugin declares no setup fields.
credentials.intro: Fields marked <secret>Secret · Vault</secret> (passwords, OAuth tokens) are stored encrypted in the vault and only shown as “stored” — never the actual value. Fields marked <config>Config</config> (enum, URL, flags) are non-sensitive and show the current selection. Type or pick a new value to overwrite, or click <trashIcon></trashIcon> to delete the value.
credentials.secretBadgeTitle: Secret — encrypted in the per-agent vault
credentials.configBadgeTitle: Config — non-sensitive value in the instance configuration
credentials.clearToDelete: clear to delete, retype to overwrite
credentials.deleteConfigOnSave: Delete config value on next save
credentials.noEntries: No selectable entries (nothing shared with the integration?).

editFromStore.hintSourceNotFound: The original Builder draft no longer exists (deleted or installed by another operator). Reverse codegen from the installed manifest.yaml arrives in B.7.
editFromStore.hintQuotaExceeded: Delete existing drafts under “Drafts” before cloning this one.

requiresWizard.intro: Before installation the following capability chain must be satisfied. Order: deepest prerequisite first, then the actual plugin.
requiresWizard.noProviderAvailable: No provider in the catalog. Please upload a suitable plugin or provide the capability server-side.
requiresWizard.providerNeedsConfig: This provider plugin needs configuration before it can be activated. Values are passed exclusively to this one plugin's vault.

upload.title: Upload a new agent package.
upload.intro: Drag a <mono>.zip</mono> here or pick it via the button. The server validates the manifest, checks peer dependencies, and registers the agent in the catalog automatically.
upload.inCatalog: <idTag>{id}</idTag> <versionTag>v{version}</versionTag> is in the catalog. Select and install it below.
upload.peersMissing: ⚠ Missing peer deps: {peers} — the agent may fail at runtime.

adminAuth.title: Authentication Providers
adminAuth.intro: Enable or disable the sign-in methods. The {envVar} env variable defines the permitted providers; here you choose which of them are currently active.

errors.selfLockout: You cannot disable the provider you are currently signed in with.
errors.lastActiveProvider: At least one provider must stay active — otherwise nobody can get in anymore.
errors.notInWhitelist: This provider is not allowed in the AUTH_PROVIDERS whitelist.

adminMcp.title: MCP Control Center
adminMcp.intro: Manage MCP servers, review scan verdicts of discovered tools, see who holds which grant, and follow the call audit log. The Builder canvas stays the place to wire grants.

servers.name: Name
servers.noTools: No tools discovered yet. Run Discover first.
servers.deleteBody: Delete server "{name}" and all of its grants? Agents lose these tools immediately.

marketplace.hint: Pick a registry and browse its catalog. Connected servers arrive disabled: run Discover, review the verdicts, then enable.
marketplace.connected: Server "{name}" created (disabled). Run Discover on the Servers tab, review verdicts, then enable it.

grants.empty: No MCP tool grants exist yet.

adminRegistries.title: Plugin Registries
adminRegistries.intro: Sources from which plugins are pulled into the store. New instances start with {hub}. The token is stored encrypted and never shown again — only whether one is set. Changes take effect without a restart.

errors.duplicate: A registry with this name already exists.
errors.invalidName: The name may only contain letters, numbers, . _ -

adminDomains.title: Plugin Domains
adminDomains.intro: Overview of all registered plugins, grouped by the {domain} declared in their {manifest}. Domains are read by the Phase 8 nudge-pipeline multi-domain trigger and used by the operator UI for cross-agent grouping. Curation (renaming / merging / hierarchies) follows with OB-78 (Phase 9 agent profiles).
adminDomains.fallbackWarning: {count, plural, one {# plugin} other {# plugins}} without a declared domain — auto-fallback to {fallbackDomain}. Add an {manifestKey} line to the manifest (e.g. {exampleOne}, {exampleTwo}) or re-upload the agent via the Builder.

adminUsage.title: Costs
adminUsage.intro: Token consumption and LLM costs per model, source and time. Captures every Anthropic call — orchestrator, sub-agents and the Haiku background tasks.

kpi.cacheHitHint: Share of input served from the warm cache

errors.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters long.
errors.emailInUse: This email is already registered as a local user.
errors.selfDisable: You cannot disable yourself. Another admin must perform this action.
errors.notLocal: Password reset only works for local accounts. Federated identities are managed by the IdP.

list.title: Users
list.intro: Local accounts and federated identities. Only local users can be created here — federated accounts appear once the user has signed in through the IdP.

edit.resetHelp: Sets the password immediately. The user is not notified — share the new password through a secure channel.
edit.dangerHelp: Deleting removes the user permanently. The audit log keeps the entry.
edit.confirmDelete: Really delete this user? This action cannot be undone.

list.title: Memory · Topics
list.intro: Clusters of all MemorableKnowledge by embedding similarity. Re-clustering is destructive: all existing topics are replaced.

recluster.heading: Re-cluster
recluster.confirm: {count} existing topics will be deleted and rebuilt. Continue?

list.empty: No topics. Start a re-cluster above.

detail.emptyMembers: No visible members (or an ACL hides them).

adminBulkPromote.title: Memory · Bulk Promotion
adminBulkPromote.intro: Scores historical turns with missing significance via Haiku and promotes the high-scoring ones as MemorableKnowledge. Idempotent — re-runs only do new work.
adminBulkPromote.confirmRun: Warning: up to {count} Haiku calls (~${cost}). Really start?
adminBulkPromote.noScorerError: No SignificanceScorer available — set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in the middleware.

adminDangerZone.title: Danger Zone · Memory Purge
adminDangerZone.intro: Deletes memory irreversibly along one axis. Everything wipes agent scratch and knowledge graph; the user / team / channel axes only affect the knowledge graph. There is no undo — always check the preview first.
adminDangerZone.note: Note: Deleting per user / team / channel only affects the knowledge graph — the agent scratch is agent-scoped.
adminDangerZone.reseedLabel: Re-seed default memories after deletion (re-seed defaults)
adminDangerZone.previewFirst: Run a preview first — deletion is only possible after that.
adminDangerZone.forbiddenError: Not authorized (403) — memory purge requires admin rights.

list.title: Memory · Possible Duplicates
list.intro: Memories that are semantically almost identical. The “Memories” tab shows MK pairs (cosine ≥ 0.95), the “Excerpts” tab shows Palaia excerpt pairs (cosine ≥ 0.97). Clicking an entry opens the side-by-side view with a resolve button.
list.confirmBulkRun: Start bulk merge detect for up to {count} memories? Cosine-only, free of charge — but the bulk scan is O(n × top-k) cosine comparisons.
list.confirmExcerptBulkRun: Bulk excerpt-duplicate detect for up to {count} excerpts? Cosine-only, free of charge.
list.bulkDetectDescription: Memories that have not had a merge check yet. Cosine-only, no LLM, practically free.
list.bulkDetectExcerptsDescription: Excerpts without a merge check (cosine ≥ 0.97). Cosine-only, free of charge.
list.emptyMemories: No duplicate candidates in this selection.
list.emptyExcerpts: No excerpt duplicates in this selection.

detail.title: Resolve duplicate
detail.cosineSummary: Cosine similarity <cosine>{value}</cosine> — memories are almost identical.
detail.reasonLabel: Reason (optional, recorded in the audit log)
detail.confirmDelete: Memory will be permanently deleted: "{summary}". Continue?

keepA.label: Keep A → delete B
keepA.description: Marks memory A as the canonical variant and permanently deletes memory B. The audit trail is preserved.

keepB.label: Keep B → delete A
keepB.description: Marks memory B as the canonical variant and permanently deletes memory A. The audit trail is preserved.

notDuplicate.label: Not a duplicate (detector overshot)
notDuplicate.description: Marks the pair as “not a duplicate”. Both memories remain unchanged. It will not be checked again.

detail.memoryUnavailable: Memory not accessible (deleted or no owner).

excerptDetail.title: Resolve excerpt duplicate
excerptDetail.cosineSummary: Cosine similarity <cosine>{value}</cosine> — excerpts are almost verbatim identical.
excerptDetail.reasonLabel: Reason (optional, recorded in the audit log)
excerptDetail.confirmDelete: Excerpt will be permanently deleted: "{text}". Continue?

keepA.label: Keep A → delete B
keepA.description: Keeps excerpt A as the canonical quote and permanently deletes excerpt B from the parent MK. The provenance audit row (delete_excerpt) is kept in memory_acl_audit.

keepB.label: Keep B → delete A
keepB.description: Keeps excerpt B as the canonical quote and permanently deletes excerpt A.

notDuplicate.label: Not a duplicate (detector overshot)
notDuplicate.description: Marks the pair as “not a duplicate”. Both excerpts remain unchanged. It will not be checked again.

excerptDetail.excerptUnavailable: Excerpt not accessible (deleted or no owner).

list.title: Memory · Inconsistencies
list.intro: Memories that are semantically related but contradict each other in content. Clicking an entry opens the side-by-side view for resolving.
list.confirmBulkRun: Start bulk detect for up to {count} memories? Up to 5 Haiku calls per memory — costs scale with the limit.
list.bulkDetectDescription: Memories that have not had an inconsistency check yet (= from before Slice 9).

detail.title: Resolve conflict
detail.reasonLabel: Reason (optional, recorded in the audit log)
detail.confirmDelete: Memory will be permanently deleted: "{summary}". Continue?

aWins.label: A correct → delete B
aWins.description: Marks memory A as correct and permanently deletes memory B. The audit trail is preserved.

bWins.label: B correct → delete A
bWins.description: Marks memory B as correct and permanently deletes memory A. The audit trail is preserved.

both.label: Both correct (different contexts)
both.description: Leaves both memories unchanged and marks the conflict as resolved. Use this when both are correct in different contexts.

dismiss.label: False positive (detector was wrong)
dismiss.description: Marks the detection as a false positive. Both memories remain unchanged. The conflict will not be checked again.

detail.memoryUnavailable: Memory not accessible (deleted or no owner).

adminKgLifecycle.quotaOverTooltip: {percent}% — will be evicted on the next GC sweep

adminKgPriorities.intro: Per-agent block/boost list for the token-budget assembler. Block drops a turn from the recall pool; boost multiplies the score by `weight` (default 1.3). `manuallyAuthored=true` additionally applies an independent ×1.3 boost — both combine.

adminMemoryBackend.title: Memory · Storage Backend
adminMemoryBackend.intro: Switches the memory storage between Postgres and In-Memory (volatile, no database). Postgres requires DATABASE_URL (Neon KG/graphPool). The choice is saved; the switch only takes effect after a restart.
adminMemoryBackend.restartPending: ⚠ A switch has been saved but is not active yet — restart required for the switch to take effect.
adminMemoryBackend.restartRequired: Restart required for the switch to take effect.
adminMemoryBackend.dbUrlRequiredError: Postgres requires DATABASE_URL (Neon KG/graphPool). Set DATABASE_URL and restart before switching to Postgres.
adminMemoryBackend.forbiddenError: Not authorized (403) — switching the backend requires admin rights.
adminMemoryBackend.noProviderError: No memory provider registered (409) — the choice cannot be saved.

page.errorDevEndpoint: Dev graph endpoint unavailable. Set DEV_ENDPOINTS_ENABLED=true in middleware/.env.
page.emptySessions: none — ask a question in the chat

filters.plansHint: Plan DAG of the selected session (#133)

canvas.emptyState: No nodes — select a session on the left or adjust the filters
canvas.help: Click = select · Double-click = load neighbors · Wheel = zoom · Drag = pan

detailPanel.manualTitle: manually captured — score boost ×1.3 in the token-budget assembler

acl.deleteConfirm: Really delete this memory? The audit trail is preserved.
acl.noOwners: No owners — the memory is only visible to admins.
acl.reasonPlaceholder: Reason (optional, recorded in the audit log)

memory.errorMiddlewareUnreachable: Middleware unreachable (localhost:3979). Is `npm run dev` running in the middleware folder?
memory.errorDevEndpointUnavailable: Dev memory endpoint unavailable. Set DEV_ENDPOINTS_ENABLED=true in middleware/.env and restart the middleware.
memory.descriptionInMemory: Live browser of the active memory store (RAM, empty after restart).
memory.descriptionPostgres: Live browser of the active memory store (persistent in Postgres).
memory.description: Live browser of the active memory store.
memory.selectEntry: Select an entry on the left…

list.subtitle: Curated knowledge — ACL-filtered to you as owner.
list.emptyAll: No memories yet. Create one via chat or POST /api/v1/memory.
list.emptyKind: No memories of kind “{kind}”. Reset the filter or pick another kind.

detail.deleteConfirm: Really delete this memory? The audit trail remains, but the item disappears from /memories.
detail.notFound: Memory not found or you are not an owner.
detail.reasonLabel: Reason for the change (optional, recorded in the audit log)
detail.excerptsSubtitle: Verbatim from the original turn — provenance anchor for the memory.
detail.excerptsEmpty: No source snippets — the memory was saved before slice 6.5 or the extractor returned no excerpts.

page.title: Cron jobs & routines.
page.intro: User-owned recurring agent invocations. They are created in the chat (manage_routine tool); here you can see everything at a glance and pause or delete.
page.emptyBody: Routines are created when a user says something like “remind me about X every Monday at 9 am” in the chat — the agent calls the {toolName} tool and creates it.

row.historyEmpty: No run history for this routine yet — as soon as it fires for the first time, a row will appear here.

actions.triggerTitle: Trigger the routine manually now — fires an agent run and delivers the result to the channel.
actions.triggerNotice: Started — the result will appear in the channel in ~30 seconds.
actions.deleteConfirm: Delete routine ''{name}''? This cannot be undone.

templateEditor.description: JSON schema: {schema}. On trigger the server renders all data + static sections itself; the LLM only fills the narrative-slot strings. Empty editor = remove the template, the routine runs on the legacy path again.
templateEditor.previewDescription: Server-side render preview without an LLM call. You provide the tool results + slot values that the renderer normally receives from C.2 + C.3 at trigger time.
templateEditor.previewNoTemplate: No template in the editor — no preview possible.
templateEditor.rawToolResultsNotObject: rawToolResults must be a JSON object.
templateEditor.slotsNotObject: slots must be a JSON object.

runTrace.empty: No run trace available (typical for errors before `runTurn` or for older runs before trace persistence was enabled).
runTrace.showMore: more ({count, plural, one {# character} other {# characters}})

runDetail.noAnswer: No answer stored (run aborted or failed before delivery).
runDetail.traceDescription: Complete agentic trace of this run — iterations, orchestrator tool calls and sub-agent invocations. Click a row to expand / collapse the node.

page.title: Platform health.
page.intro: Runtime, persistence, backup. Metadata only — no secrets.
page.errorUnknownLoad: Unknown error while loading the vault status.
